name: Sunrise Banks, National Association
description_short: Sunrise Banks is a certified B Corporation community bank operating in Minnesota and South Dakota, offering mortgages, savings products, credit building, and business banking with a focus on financial empowerment and social responsibility.
description_long:
Sunrise Banks was founded in 1985 by David Reiling, a social entrepreneur focused on fintech innovation and sustainable economic growth. The bank operates with physical locations in Minneapolis, St. Paul, Minnesota, and Sioux Falls, South Dakota, positioning itself as a values-driven alternative to traditional banks. The institution has grown to serve communities across multiple states while maintaining its core mission of financial wellness and ethical banking practices.

Sunrise Banks offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (currently at 3.80% APY on 6-month CDs), home mortgages with down payment assistance, home equity lines of credit, credit builder loans, debit/ATM cards, credit cards, and digital banking services. For business customers, they provide business checking, savings accounts, treasury management, and customized cash flow solutions. They also offer specialized mortgage options including Net Zero Financing for energy-efficient properties and have reduced closing costs on HELOCs through June 2026 and mortgages through July 2026.

Sunrise Banks distinguishes itself through multiple certifications and memberships that verify its ethical stance: certified B Corporation status, certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) designation, and membership in the Global Alliance for Banking on Values. The bank explicitly markets itself as "socially responsible" and emphasizes community engagement, financial empowerment, and generational wealth building. Their focus on accessibility includes credit builder products for those establishing credit history and down payment assistance programs for homebuyers with varying financial situations.

As a traditional community bank, Sunrise Banks may have limited geographic reach compared to national banks, with operations concentrated in Minnesota and South Dakota. While their certifications and mission-driven approach appeal to values-conscious consumers, they operate as a full-service bank rather than specializing in any particular product category. Customers should verify whether their specific location is served and whether product offerings match their needs, as community banks typically offer narrower product ranges than larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home mortgages with down payment assistance
Treasury management and cash flow services
Digital banking and online account management
Net Zero Financing for energy-efficient properties
pros: Certified B Corporation with verified ethical banking standards and CDFI designation
Competitive CD rates (3.80% APY on 6-month CDs with $500 minimum deposit)
Mortgage closing cost assistance ($500 off through July 31, 2026) and down payment assistance programs
Specialized products like Net Zero Financing for energy-efficient home improvements
Credit builder loans specifically designed for financial empowerment and credit establishment
No closing costs on HELOCs through June 30, 2026, offering flexible borrowing alternatives
Membership in Global Alliance for Banking on Values demonstrates commitment to sustainable practices
cons: Geographic limitation: only operates in Minneapolis, St. Paul, and Sioux Falls areas, not a national bank
Limited branch network compared to major national banks may restrict ATM and in-person service access
No specific APR information provided on website for credit cards or other credit products, making rate comparison difficult
As a community bank, likely offers fewer specialized products than large financial institutions
No mention of fee structures for checking accounts, overdraft fees, or other standard banking charges

contact.address: 5105 S Crossing Pl, Sioux Falls, SD 57108

best_for: Values-conscious consumers in Minnesota and South Dakota seeking ethical banking alternatives
Homebuyers looking for mortgage options with closing cost assistance and down payment support
Individuals building credit history who want a dedicated credit builder loan product
Small business owners needing community-focused banking relationships and treasury management services
diagnosis: Sunrise Banks is best for consumers in Minnesota and South Dakota who prioritize ethical banking, community impact, and financial empowerment alongside traditional banking services. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional community bank, not a national institution, so customers outside their service areas cannot access their products or branches.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 5105 S Crossing Pl, Sioux Falls, SD 57108
